title: CARF Benchmark Manifest Entry
description: Per-benchmark provenance and evidence-grading metadata. Loaded by benchmarks/reports/realism.py and gated by benchmarks/reports/check_result_evidence.py --strict-manifest.

category.description: Benchmark category: core, governance, security, compliance, sustainability, ux, industry, performance, monitoring, competitive, causal.

dataset_profile.description: Origin of inputs. 'real' = production-derived; 'hybrid' = real seeds with synthetic perturbation; 'synthetic' = fully generated.

data_source.description: Concrete origin of the dataset: file path, generator script, dataset URL, or production extract identifier. Required for evidence gating.

ground_truth_type.description: How ground truth was established. 'none' is allowed only for descriptive benchmarks (e.g., latency).

llm_provider.description: LLM(s) used in-loop, e.g. 'deepseek/v3.1', 'openai/gpt-4o-mini', 'multi:deepseek+anthropic', or 'n/a' for non-LLM benchmarks.

seed.description: Concrete seed value used to drive deterministic execution. Must be set; seedless benchmarks should record the randomisation policy in source_reference and lower their evidence_grade accordingly.

uses_mock.description: True when any production dependency (LLM, retriever, Neo4j, governance, HumanLayer) is replaced by a mock or stub during the benchmark run.

uses_fallback.description: True when the benchmark's success metric counts results produced via documented fallback paths (e.g., TF-IDF RAG when LightRAG unavailable, statistical Bayesian fallback, mock HumanLayer approval).

sample_size.description: Number of independent measurement units summarised by the headline metric. May differ from `rows` when multiple rows aggregate to one measurement.

confidence_interval.description: Method/width of the confidence interval reported with the headline metric, e.g. '95% Wilson', 'bootstrap-1000', 'paired-t-95'. null only when the benchmark is purely descriptive.

repro_command.description: Exact command (or command sequence) that reproduces the result file used by generate_report.py.

evidence_grade.description:
Strength classification used in CARF reporting:
  - validated: real or production-mirrored data, baseline comparator, no fallback masking, deterministic, replicated.
  - synthetic-only: useful sanity check but ground truth is generator-defined; cannot stand as production evidence on its own.
  - stubbed: relies on mocks or fallback paths that mask real-system behaviour.
  - aspirational: claim with target threshold but no evaluation logic or no result file yet.
  - needs-independent-replication: internally validated but never reproduced outside this repository.
